articles.title: Jed McCaleb’s XRP bag is almost gone, Ethereum’s difficulty bomb delayed, and FTX inks deal with BlockFi: Hodler’s Digest, June 26-July 2
Crypto Firms In The US Have Paid Over $3 Billion In Fines With The SEC Being The Largest Collector
articles.description: Crypto firms have paid over $3 billion in fines to US authorities since Satoshi Nakamoto launched Bitcoin. BlockFi was slammed with the largest fine after it paid $100 million early in the year. Payment of fines suggests that the cryptocurrency industry is not as unregulated as critics paint it to be. The US Securities and […]

articles.title: Circle’s CEO Quells USDC Fears, States the Company Is Financially Strong
articles.description: Summary: Circle’s CEO has published a Twitter thread quelling fears and rumors that USDC is on the brink of collapse. He added that it is understandable why the crypto community would worry about USDC, given the recent collapse of several crypto projects. He reaffirms Circle is committed to being transparent with its reserves fully backed, and Circle is at the strongest it has ever been...

articles.title: KuCoin CEO Says Ignore FUD, Denies Existence of a Plan To Halt Crypto Withdrawals
articles.description: On Saturday, Johnny Lyu, Co-Founder and CEO of crypto exchange KuCoin, said that there was “no ‘immense suffer’ from any ‘coin collapse’, “no plan to halt withdrawal,” and that “everything on KuCoin is operating well.” KuCoin, which was launched in September 2017, has its operational headquarters in Seychelles. It “offers over 700 digital assets, and […]

articles.title: KuCoin CEO Denies Withdrawal Freeze Rumors
articles.description: The rumors surfaced hours after Voyager Digital announced it was freezing customer withdrawals amid the ongoing Three Arrows Capital crisis. KuCoin Operating as Normal, CEO Insists Rumors that KuCoin could...
